meaning: n.阿芝台克人,阿芝台克语adj.阿芝台克人的
examples: But the Aztec civilization back in the thirteenth century is basically where Mexico city is today.
Although the mysteries of the Aztec, Mayan, and Inca civilizations have been known for centuries, now the public is also becoming aware of unusual, paranormal phenomena in countries such as Peru.
These floating gardens are proof that the Aztec invented hydroponic farming.
By the third glass, I had acquired a taste for the drink of the Aztec gods.
Magnificent Aztec-themed art deco buildings line the restaurant-thronged river that winds through the downtown district.
